_note_mapping: HyperLiquid Mode B (UserSigned) REST exchange action `HyperliquidTransaction:UsdSend`, an OFF-CHAIN L1 action authorized by an eth_signTypedData_v4 signature (domain.name=HyperliquidSignTransaction, version=1, verifyingContract=0x0, chainId=signatureChainId). Its fields are L1 semantics — `amount` is a DECIMAL STRING ('100.0', not U256) and `destination` is an L1 identifier string — which the 8-domain ActionBody schema cannot faithfully represent. A token.erc20_transfer mapping would be a MISLABEL (amount->0, token->0x0 = data loss), so this routes to best-effort `Unknown`: the WIN is ROUTING (the user gets 'recognized: HyperLiquid UsdSend signature' instead of no_adapter); the STRUCTURED representation (destination/amount) requires a new off-chain-exchange ActionBody variant = deferred schema enhancement (B.3 key limitation, OUT OF SCOPE). target=0x0 sentinel (off-chain sign has no contract target); calldata='0x' (sigs have no calldata); value='0'.
